title: podman machine: enable hypervisor time sync (--timesync) for vfkit and krunkit on macOS
body:
### Feature request description

Enable hypervisor time synchronization for Podman Machine on macOS by wiring the existing `--timesync` support in vfkit and krunkit into Podman’s VM startup.

This relates to ongoing host vs guest clock skew on macOS (sleep/resume, and sub-second differences that break tests or strict time ordering). Related reports: #26185, #27293. The krunkit gained `--timesync` (vfkit-compatible) with the stack described in PR #28308. Reviewers noted Podman still needs code to pass that flag so users actually benefit.

### Suggest potential solution

- In `pkg/machine` (shared Apple VM start path), when enabled, pass `--timesync` with a chosen vsock port to both `vfkit` and `krunkit`, consistent with [vfkit’s time sync behavior](https://github.com/crc-org/vfkit/blob/main/doc/usage.md) and krunkit’s matching flag.
- Work with `podman-machine-os` as needed so the guest runs `qemu-guest-agent` (or equivalent) on that vsock port if the image does not already provide it (see discussion in [#26185](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26185)).
- Update man pages (podman-machine-init, podman-machine-set, troubleshooting: sleep/resume, guest vs host time).

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

> I used Cursor to figure out what needed to be done and to help with the writing.

comments.body: I don't have the capacity at the moment to implement this. However, if someone else wants to implement it I would be more than happy to review it or answer krunkit/libkrun related questions.

@pflajszer For the `--timesync` flag to work properly with vfkit/krunkit, the podman-machine-os image needs the qemu-guest-agent running inside the VM.
There's already an open PR for this:
https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/175
I'm currently testing and rebasing it. Once that's done, we can mark it ready for review to get it merged.

I don't have the necessary permissions to push to this PR: https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/175
I have suggested some changes that would make it work

You might have to take over the PR. I haven't seen @ninja-quokka in a while in upstream. Is it okay @ninja-quokka, to take over that PR? 

@vyasgun Let's wait a few days for a response. If @ninja-quokka don't reply, please take over the PR.
Yep, this could indirectly help, but I have hopes that https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/pull/627 will fix this for good.
Nice, I am happy to hear that.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
